# rm: hard-delete; reject-if-referenced; --force; not-found exit 4
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init --template hierarchy
stdout 'initialized'

exec mtt add --type epic 'E'
stdout 'created e1'
exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 'A'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 'B'
stdout 'created t2'

# t2 depends on t1
exec mtt dep add t2 t1
stdout 'now t2 depends on t1'

# rm t1 is rejected (t2 depends on it) and t1 survives
! exec mtt rm t1
stderr 'referenced by t2'
exec mtt show t1
stdout 't1  task  \[tbd\]'

# --force deletes t1 despite the reference (forces who+why since t5)
exec mtt rm t1 --force --who a --why 'stale'
stdout 'removed t1'
! exec mtt show t1
stderr 'not found'

# t2 now has a dangling blocker → not ready
exec mtt ready
! stdout 't2'

# removing an absent task exits non-zero (not-found → code 4, asserted in a unit test)
! exec mtt rm t9
stderr 'not found'

# rm t2 (now unreferenced) succeeds
exec mtt rm t2
stdout 'removed t2'
exec mtt list
! stdout 't2'

# rm <id> --json emits the removed task object (mirrors add --json) — t45
exec mtt add --no-parent 'to delete'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt rm t1 --json
stdout '"id": "t1"'
stdout '"status"'
! stdout 'removed t1'

# bulk selector --exclude-tag (c16): de-noise while sparing tagged tasks
# (the freed max id is re-minted after the deletes above, so these are t1..t3)
exec mtt add --no-parent 'keep me #keep'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt add --no-parent 'noise one'
stdout 'created t2'
exec mtt add --no-parent 'noise two'
stdout 'created t3'
exec mtt rm --type task --exclude-tag keep --dry-run
stdout 't2'
stdout 't3'
! stdout 't1'
exec mtt rm --type task --exclude-tag keep
stdout 'removed 2 task\(s\): t2, t3'
exec mtt list
stdout 'keep me'
! stdout 'noise'
